expr.error_message: Assets must be ordered by ID
assert target is match for conditions

documentation.description:
Bootstrap the pool: create pool token and opt in to both trading assets.
Requires a seed payment to fund the contract's min-balance.

args.assetA: - First asset in the trading pair (must have lower ID)
args.assetB: - Second asset in the trading pair (must have higher ID)
args.seed: - Payment transaction to fund the contract's min-balance

documentation.returns: The newly created pool token asset ID

expr.error_message: assert target is match for conditions
assert target is match for conditions

documentation.description:
Add liquidity by depositing both assets.
First deposit: liquidity = sqrt(amountA) × sqrt(amountB) − MIN_LOCKED.
Subsequent deposits: liquidity proportional to existing reserves.

args.depositA: - Asset transfer transaction depositing asset A
args.depositB: - Asset transfer transaction depositing asset B

documentation.returns: The amount of pool tokens minted

expr.error_message: assert target is match for conditions

documentation.description:
Remove liquidity by returning pool tokens.
Proportional amounts of both assets are withdrawn.

args.poolDeposit: - Asset transfer transaction returning pool tokens

expr.error_message: assert target is match for conditions

documentation.description:
Swap one asset for the other using the constant-product formula.
Deposit asset A to receive asset B, or vice versa.
A 0.3% fee is deducted from the input.

args.deposit: - Asset transfer transaction depositing the input asset

documentation.returns: The amount of the output asset sent to the swapper

documentation.description:
Withdraw excess Algo balance above the minimum required.
Demonstrates itxn.payment and Account.minBalance.

Read-only view: pool reserves, liquidity, and invariant sqrt.
Demonstrates biguint math and op.bsqrt for computing sqrt(k).
documentation.returns: The current pool state with reserves and total liquidity
